questions.question:
​​​​​​Consider five random variables \( U, V, W, X \), and \( Y \) whose joint distribution satisfies:

\[
P(U, V, W, X, Y)=P(U) P(V) P(W \mid U, V) P(X \mid W) P(Y \mid W)
\]

Which ONE of the following statements is FALSE?

 



GATE DS&AI 2024 | Question: 14
options: \( Y \) is conditionally independent of \( V \) given \( W \)
\( X \) is conditionally independent of \( U \) given \( W \)
\( U \) and \( V \) are conditionally independent given \( W \)
\( Y \) and \( X \) are conditionally independent given \( W \)
questions.correct_answer: \( U \) and \( V \) are conditionally independent given \( W \)

questions.question:
Given the following Bayesian Network consisting of four Bernoulli random variables and the associated conditional probability tables:


\begin{array}{|c|c|}
\hline & P(\cdot) \\
\hline U=0 & 0.5 \\
\hline U=1 & 0.5 \\
\hline
\end{array}
\begin{array}{|c|c|c|}
\hline & P(V=0 \mid \cdot) & P(V=1 \mid \cdot) \\
\hline U=0 & 0.5 & 0.5 \\
\hline U=1 & 0.5 & 0.5 \\
\hline
\end{array}
\begin{array}{|c|c|c|}
\hline & P(W=0 \mid \cdot) & P(W=1 \mid \cdot) \\
\hline U=0 & 1 & 0 \\
\hline U=1 & 0 & 1 \\
\hline
\end{array}
\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|}
\hline & & P(Z=0 \mid \cdot) & P(Z=1 \mid \cdot) \\
\hline V=0 & W=0 & 0.5 & 0.5 \\
\hline V=0 & W=1 & 1 & 0 \\
\hline V=1 & W=0 & 1 & 0 \\
\hline V=1 & W=1 & 0.5 & 0.5 \\
\hline
\end{array}

The value of \( P(U=1, V=1, W=1, Z=1)= \_\_\_\_\_\_\_ \) (rounded off to three decimal places).

 



GATE DS&AI 2024 | Question: 54
